title: Interactive Exothermic and Endothermic Energy Diagrams
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Adjust activation energy, enthalpy change, and catalyst presence to compare exothermic vs. endothermic energy profiles in real time.

learningObjectives: Interpret potential energy diagrams to identify Ea and ΔH.
Explain how catalysts affect Ea without changing ΔH.
prerequisites: Definition of exothermic vs endothermic reactions

keyQuestions: How do Ea and ΔH differ between exothermic and endothermic reactions?
What does a catalyst change on an energy diagram?
commonMisconceptions: Exothermic reactions have zero activation energy.
assessmentOpportunities: Students record Ea/ΔH for different settings
Learners explain the effects of toggling the catalyst
